bullet_points: Load the dataset from the provided CSV path.
Print shape, column names, and data types.
Check for missing values (NaNs), infinite values, and duplicate material_id entries.
Print the first and last 5 rows.
Count unique values for crystal_system, magnetic_ordering, A_site, B_site, and spacegroup_symbol.
Verify nelements is always 3, band_gap >= 0, and energy_above_hull >= -0.01 eV/atom.
Compute summary statistics (mean, std, min, max, percentiles) for all numeric columns.
Compute value counts and percentages for boolean (theoretical, is_stable, is_metal, is_gap_direct, is_magnetic) and categorical columns.
Identify outliers using the IQR method for volume, band_gap, K_VRH, G_VRH, formation_energy_per_atom, energy_above_hull, tau, and mu.
Calculate and print a correlation matrix for physically meaningful pairs (structural vs. thermodynamic, elemental vs. band gap).
Generate a multi-panel figure:
Bar chart of crystal_system frequency.
Histograms for formation_energy_per_atom, band_gap, volume, and tau.
Distributions of elastic properties (K_VRH, G_VRH, poisson_ratio, elastic_anisotropy) for the 215 available entries.
Scatter plot of tau vs. energy_above_hull colored by crystal_system.
Heatmap of the correlation matrix from Step 2.
Save all plots as high-resolution PNG files in the designated directory.
Review findings from the engineer's reports.
Compile clarifying questions regarding:
Representativeness of the 215-material elastic subset.
Potential DFT convergence artifacts in energy_above_hull.
Ambiguity in site assignment due to A/B-site element overlap.
Synthesize all findings into a comprehensive report.
Address: property ranges/distributions, metal/insulator/semiconductor fractions, tau vs. crystal system relationships, skewness/heavy tails for ML, and a data completeness summary table.
Provide recommendations for future modeling.
